techs.name: Foraging
techs.quote: 'A foraging wild creature, intent above all upon survival, is as strong as the grass.' - Richard Adams

techs.name: Agriculture
techs.quote: 'Where tillage begins, other arts follow. The farmers therefore are the founders of human civilization.' - Daniel Webster

techs.name: Stone Tools
techs.quote: 'We shape our tools, and thereafter our tools shape us.' - Marshall McLuhan

techs.name: Sailing
techs.quote: 'He who commands the sea has command of everything.' - Themistocles

techs.name: Pottery
techs.quote: 'No man ever wetted clay and then left it, as if there would be bricks by chance and fortune.' - Plutarch

techs.name: Animal Husbandry
techs.quote: 'We can judge the heart of a man by his treatment of animals.' - Immanuel Kant

techs.name: Archery
uniques: [+50]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <for [Macedonia] Civilizations> <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Listen to me you suitors... I will bring out the mighty bow of Ulysses, and whomsoever of you shall string it most easily and send his arrow through each one of twelve axes, him will I follow and quit this house of my lawful husband, so goodly, and so abounding in wealth.' - Penelope

techs.name: Mining
uniques: [+100]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <for [Macedonia] Civilizations> <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'The meek shall inherit the Earth, but not its mineral rights.' - J. Paul Getty

techs.name: Optics
techs.quote: 'Set your course by the stars, not by the lights of every passing ship.' - Omar Bradley

techs.name: Calendar
techs.quote: 'When the Pleiades, daughters of Atlas, are rising, begin your harvest, and your ploughing when they are going to set.' - Hesiod

techs.name: Writing
techs.quote: 'Spoken words are the symbols of mental experience and written words are the symbols of spoken words.' - Aristotle

techs.name: Trapping
techs.quote: 'Until lions have their own historians, tales of the hunt will always glorify the hunter.' - west african proverb

techs.name: The Wheel
techs.quote: 'Negative and pessimistic views of technology have always existed. I can just imagine some pessimistic Sumerian in 3500 B.C. screaming about the evils of the wheel.' - Gray Scott

techs.name: Masonry
techs.quote: 'Who built Thebes of the seven gates? / In the books you will find the names of kings. / Did the kings haul up the lumps of rock?' - Bertolt Brecht

techs.name: Bronze Working
uniques: [+200]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <for [Macedonia] Civilizations> <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Here Hector entered, with a spear eleven cubits long in his hand; the bronze point gleamed in front of him, and was fastened to the shaft of the spear by a ring of gold.' - Homer

techs.name: Fermentation
techs.quote: 'A bottle of wine contains more philosophy than all the books in the world.'- Louis Pasteur

techs.name: Spirituality
techs.quote: 'Impotence of the exploited classes in their struggle against the exploiters just as inevitably gives rise to the belief in a better life after death as impotence of the savage in his battle with nature gives rise to belief in gods, devils, miracles, and the like.' - Vladimir Lenin

techs.name: Horseback Riding
techs.quote: 'It is easy to conquer the world on horseback. But to dismount and govern? That is the hardest task.' - Genghis Khan

techs.name: Mathematics
techs.quote: 'Mathematics reveals its secrets only to those who approach it with pure love, for its own beauty.' - Archimedes

techs.name: Construction
techs.quote: 'If our designs for private houses are to be correct, we must at the outset take note of the countries and climates in which they are built.' - Marcus Vitruvius Pollio

techs.name: Shipbuilding
techs.quote: 'Joyfully to the breeze royal Odysseus spread his sail, and with his rudder skillfully he steered.' - Homer

techs.name: Drama and Poetry
techs.quote: 'Art is not a mirror held up to reality but a hammer with which to shape it.' - Bertolt Brecht

techs.name: Philosophy
techs.quote: 'All men are intellectuals, but not all men have in society the function of intellectuals.' - Antonio Gramsci

techs.name: Imperial Games
techs.quote: 'The People who once upon a time handed out military command, high civil office, legions — everything, now restrains itself and anxiously hopes for just two things: bread and circuses.' - Juvenal

techs.name: Currency
uniques: [+300]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Money is a new form of slavery. Distinguishable from the old simply by the fact that it is impersonal, that there is no human relation between master and slave.' - Leo Tolstoy

techs.name: Engineering
techs.quote: 'Give me a place to stand and with a lever I will move the whole world.' - Archimedes

techs.name: Iron Working
techs.quote: 'Do not wait to strike til the iron is hot, but make it hot by striking.' - William Butler Yeats

techs.name: Compass
techs.quote: 'I find the great thing in this world is not so much where we stand, as in what direction we are moving.' - Oliver Wendell Holmes

techs.name: Theology
techs.quote: 'The foundation of irreligious criticism is: Man makes religion, religion does not make man. Religion is, indeed, the self-consciousness and self-esteem of man who has either not yet won through to himself, or has already lost himself again.' - Karl Marx

techs.name: Civil Service
techs.quote: 'The state is nothing but an instrument of oppression of one class by another - no less so in a democratic republic than in a monarchy.' - Friedrich Engels

techs.name: Guilds
techs.quote: 'The towns banded together for protection of life and industry, and thus developed the guild of the Middle Ages. Relieved from the fear of free-booting barons, no less dangerous than the hordes of organized robbers, these guilds grew populous and powerful.' - Helen Campbell

techs.name: Military Tactics
uniques: Comment [+10% Strength to Military units adjacent to another Military unit]
[{non-air} {Military}] units gain the [Formation I] promotion <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Tactics mean doing what you can with what you have.' - Saul Alinsky

techs.name: Metal Casting
techs.quote: 'When pieces of bronze or gold or iron break, the metal-smith welds them together again in the fire, and the bond is established.' - Sri Guru Granth Sahib

techs.name: Education
uniques: Allied City-States provide [Science] equal to [50]% of what they produce for themselves
techs.quote: 'It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.' - Aristotle

techs.name: Distillation
techs.quote: 'For art to exist, for any sort of aesthetic activity or perception to exist,a certain physiological precondition is indispensable: intoxication'- Friedrich Nietzsche

techs.name: Chivalry
techs.quote: 'Of noble knights and ladies, arms, and love, / Of chivalry, and noble deeds I sing...' - Ludovico Ariosto

techs.name: Machinery
techs.quote: 'Remember that people break down too, not just machinery.' - Gregory Benford

techs.name: Physics
techs.quote: 'Measure what is measurable, and make measurable what is not so.' - Galileo Galilei

techs.name: Steel
techs.quote: 'There never was a good knife made of bad steel.' - Benjamin Franklin

techs.name: Astronomy
uniques: Enables [Embarked] units to enter ocean tiles
techs.quote: 'When the land is not enough, set sail. The ocean shall reveal more land.' - Dyah Gitarja

techs.name: Acoustics
techs.quote: 'Music expresses that which cannot be put into words.' - Victor Hugo

techs.name: Mechanics
techs.quote: 'When you want to know how things really work, study them when they're coming apart.' - William Gibson

techs.name: Banking
techs.quote: 'A bank is a place that will lend you money if you can prove you don't need it.' - Bob Hope

techs.name: Printing Press
techs.quote: 'The press does many things and serves many functions but its major role, its irreducible responsibility, is to continually recreate a view of reality supportive of existing social and economic class power.' - Michael Parenti

techs.name: Gunpowder
techs.quote: 'Political power grows out of the barrel of a gun.' - Mao Zedong

techs.name: Navigation
techs.quote: 'A nation’s greatness is measured by its navy.' - Alfred Thayer Mahan

techs.name: Architecture
techs.quote: 'We shape our buildings; thereafter they shape us.' - Winston Churchill

techs.name: Economics
uniques: Enables conversion of city production to [Gold]
techs.quote: 'In studying economic history, we tend to systematically ignore the role of violence as well as the absolutely central role that war and slavery played in creating and shaping the basic institutions of what we now call 'the economy'.' - David Graeber

techs.name: Metallurgy
techs.quote: 'There are three things extremely hard: steel, a diamond, and to know one's self.' - Benjamin Franklin

techs.name: Siege Tactics
techs.quote: 'The lowest is to attack a city. Siege of a city is only done as a last resort.' - Sun Tzu

techs.name: Chemistry
uniques: Enables conversion of city production to [Science]
techs.quote: 'In the natural sciences, and particularly in chemistry, generalities must come after the detailed knowledge of each fact and not before it.' - Joseph Louis Gay-Lussac

techs.name: Archaeology
techs.quote: 'Those who cannot remember the past are condemned to repeat it.' - George Santayana

techs.name: Scientific Theory
techs.quote: 'Claims that cannot be tested, assertions immune to disproof are veridically worthless, whatever value they may have in inspiring us or exciting our sense of wonder.' - Carl Sagan

techs.name: Urban Planning
uniques: Comment [+10% Production when constructing pre-industrial buildings in all cities]
[+10]% Production when constructing [Prehistoric era] buildings [in all cities] <hidden from users>
[+10]% Production when constructing [Ancient era] buildings [in all cities] <hidden from users>
[+10]% Production when constructing [Classical era] buildings [in all cities] <hidden from users>
[+10]% Production when constructing [Medieval era] buildings [in all cities] <hidden from users>
[+10]% Production when constructing [Renaissance era] buildings [in all cities] <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'A common mistake that people make when trying to design something completely foolproof is to underestimate the ingenuity of complete fools.' - Douglas Adams

techs.name: Industrialization
techs.quote: 'The more the division of labour and the application of machinery extend, the more does competition extend among the workers, the more do their wages shrink together.' - Karl Marx

techs.name: Rifling
techs.quote: 'Wars may be fought with weapons, but they are won by men. It is the spirit of the men who follow and of the man who leads that gains the victory.' - George S. Patton

techs.name: Military Science
techs.quote: 'If you know the enemy and know yourself, you need not fear the result of a hundred battles.' - Sun Tzu

techs.name: Fertilizer
techs.quote: 'Remember to feed your people. Soldiers' bellies are not satisfied with empty promises and hopes.' - Peter the Great

techs.name: Biology
uniques: Comment [Military units heal +5 HP when healing with an additional +5 HP when adjacent to another Military unit]
[{non-air} {Military}] units gain the [Medic] promotion <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'If the brain were so simple we could understand it, we would be so simple we couldn't.' - Lyall Watson

techs.name: Electricity
uniques: [+300]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Invention is the most important product of man's creative brain. The ultimate purpose is the complete mastery of mind over the material world, the harnessing of human nature to human needs.' - Nikola Tesla

techs.name: Refining
techs.quote: 'By the fall of 1918, it was clear that a nation's prosperity, even its very survival, depended on securing a safe, abundant supply of cheap oil.' - Albert Marrin

techs.name: Steam Power
techs.quote: 'In acquiring new productive forces men... change all their social relations. The hand-mill gives you society with the feudal lord; the steam-mill, society with the industrial capitalist.' - Karl Marx

techs.name: Dynamite
techs.quote: 'Increased knowledge clearly implies increased responsibility.' - Nicolaas Bloembergen

techs.name: Refrigeration
techs.quote: 'Why do some people have to be miserably poor in order that others can be extravagantly rich? I speak for all the children in the world who don't even have a piece of bread.' - Fidel Castro

techs.name: Radio
techs.quote: 'The power of radio is not that it speaks to millions, but that it speaks intimately and privately to each one of those millions.' - Hallie Flanagan

techs.name: Financial Capital
uniques: Comment [+2 [Gold] for every Luxury resource you own]
[Gold] cost of purchasing items in cities [-10]%
techs.quote: 'Imperialism is the export of capital to the sources of raw materials, the frenzied struggle for monopolist possession of these sources, the struggle for a re-division of the already divided world, a struggle waged with particular fury by new financial groups and Powers seeking a 'place in the sun' against the old groups and Powers, which cling tenaciously to what they have seized.' - Joseph Stalin

techs.name: Replaceable Parts
techs.quote: 'Nothing is particularly hard if you divide it into small jobs.' - Henry Ford

techs.name: Flight
techs.quote: 'Our own growing belief that man might nevertheless learn to fly was based on the idea that while thousands of the most dissimilar body structures, such as insects, fish, reptiles, birds and mammals, were flying every day at pleasure, it was reasonable to suppose that man might also fly.' - Wright Brothers

techs.name: Combustion
uniques: Religion naturally spreads to cities [+3] tiles away
techs.quote: 'In nature nothing is created, nothing is lost, everything is transformed.' - Antoine Lavoisier

techs.name: Plastics
uniques: [+300]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'Not only are plastics polluting our oceans and waterways and killing marine life – it’s in all of us and we can’t escape consuming plastics.' - Marco Lambertini

techs.name: Sociology
uniques: [+8 Culture, +8 Science] <in cities with at least [4] [Specialists]>
Comment [May start a Revolution when below -10 [Happiness]]
techs.quote: 'The philosophers have only interpreted the world, in various ways. The point, however, is to change it.' - Karl Marx

techs.name: Electronics
techs.quote: 'There's a basic principle about consumer electronics: it gets more powerful all the time and it gets cheaper all the time.' - Trip Hawkins

techs.name: Cultural Hegemony
uniques: Influence of all other civilizations with all city-states degrades [25]% faster
Each city founded increases culture cost of policies [25]% less than normal
Each city founded increases Science cost of Technologies [25]% less than normal
techs.quote: 'Ideas and opinions are not spontaneously born in each individual brain: they have had a centre of formation, or irradiation, of dissemination, of persuasion-a group of men, or a single individual even, which has developed them and presented them in the political form of current reality.' - Antonio Gramsci

techs.name: Ballistics
techs.quote: 'Just because you do not take an interest in politics doesn't mean politics won't take an interest in you.' - Pericles

techs.name: Combined Arms
techs.quote: 'War is the continuation of politics by other means.' - Carl von Clausewitz

techs.name: Pharmaceuticals
techs.quote: 'In nothing do men more nearly approach the gods than in giving health to men.' - Cicero

techs.name: Ecology
uniques: [+300]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'The essence of capitalism is to turn nature into commodities and commodities into capital. The live green earth is transformed into dead gold bricks, with luxury items for the few and toxic slag heaps for the many. The glittering mansion overlooks a vast sprawl of shanty towns, wherein a desperate, demoralized humanity is kept in line with drugs, television, and armed force.' - Michael Parenti

techs.name: Atomic Theory
uniques: Comment [Resting points with City-States will raise by triple the Strength level of each of the nuclear devices in your possession]
techs.quote: 'I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds.' - J. Robert Oppenheimer

techs.name: Radar
techs.quote: 'Vision is the art of seeing things invisible.' - Jonathan Swift

techs.name: Information Theory
uniques: Production to [Gold] conversion in cities changed by [+100]%
Production to [Science] conversion in cities changed by [+100]%
techs.quote: 'An individual tends to absorb the surrounding spirit and to radiate the acquired lifestyle and worldview to anyone around, not just to a select friend.' - Andrej Kolmogorov

techs.name: Advanced Espionage
techs.quote: 'One spy in the right place is worth twenty thousand men in the field.' - Napoleon Bonaparte

techs.name: Advanced Urbanism
techs.quote: 'But when the social entity grows large, becomes a megalopolis, a state, a federation, then the governing machine grows remote, impersonal, even inhuman. It takes money from us for purposes we do not seem to sanction; it treats us as abstract statistics; it controls an army; it supports a police force whose function does not always appear to be protective.' - Anthony Burgess

techs.name: Environmentalism
uniques: [+300]% weight to this choice for AI decisions <hidden from users>
techs.quote: 'It is important to leave a better land for our descendants.' - Theodore Roosevelt

techs.name: Nuclear Fission
techs.quote: 'Can a nation be free if it oppresses other nations? It cannot.' - Vladimir Lenin

techs.name: Rocketry
techs.quote: 'Every gun that is made, every warship launched, every rocket fired, signifies in the final sense a theft from those who hunger and are not fed, those who are cold and are not clothed.' - Dwight D. Eisenhower

techs.name: Cybernetics
uniques: [+1 Science] from every specialist [in all cities]
techs.quote: 'We should be on our guard not to overestimate science and scientific methods when it is a question of human problems; and we should not assume that experts are the only ones who have a right to express themselves on questions affecting the organization of society.' - Albert Einstein

techs.name: Computers
techs.quote: 'A computer can never be held accountable, therefore a computer must never make a management decision.' - caution from 1917 IBM presentation

techs.name: Telecommunications
techs.quote: 'When people speak of the ideas that revolutionise society, they do but express that fact that within the old society the elements of a new one have been created, and that the dissolution of the old ideas keeps even pace with the dissolution of the old conditions of existence.' - Karl Marx & Friedrich Engels

techs.name: Mobile Tactics
techs.quote: 'All men can see these tactics whereby I conquer, but what none can see is the strategy out of which victory is evolved.' - Sun Tzu

techs.name: Advanced Ballistics
techs.quote: 'The nuclear arms race is like two sworn enemies standing waist deep in gasoline, one with three matches, the other with five.' – Carl Sagan

techs.name: Satellites
techs.quote: 'Looking at the earth from afar you realize it is too small for conflict and just big enough for co-operation.' - Yuri Gagarin

techs.name: Robotics
techs.quote: '1. A robot may not injure a human being or, through inaction, allow a human being to come to harm. 2. A robot must obey any orders given to it by human beings, except when such orders would conflict with the First Law. 3. A robot must protect its own existence as long as such protection does not conflict with the First or Second Law.' - Isaac Asimov

techs.name: Lasers
techs.quote: 'A laser is a weak source of energy... But with a laser, you can drill a hole in a diamond or wipe out cancer.' - Al Ries

techs.name: Globalization
uniques: Gifts of Gold to City-States generate [25]% more Influence
techs.quote: 'Designed to leave the world's economic destiny at the mercy of bankers and multinational corporations, Globalization is a logical extension of imperialism, a victory of empire over republic, international finance capital over democracy.' - Michael Parenti

techs.name: Particle Physics
techs.quote: 'If you think you understand quantum mechanics, you don’t understand quantum mechanics.' - Richard Feynman

techs.name: Nanotechnology
techs.quote: 'Nanotechnology is the idea that we can create devices and machines all the way down to the nanometer scale, which is a billionth of a meter, about half the width of a human DNA molecule.' - Paul McEuen

techs.name: Stealth
techs.quote: 'Keep the enemy in the dark about where and when our forces will attack.' - Mao Zedong

techs.name: Nuclear Fusion
techs.quote: 'I would like nuclear fusion to become a practical power source. It would provide an inexhaustible supply of energy, without pollution or global warming.' - Stephen Hawking

techs.name: Advanced AI
techs.quote: 'By far the greatest danger of Artificial Intelligence is that people conclude too early that they understand it.' - Eliezer Yudkowsky

techs.name: Future Tech
techs.quote: 'Any sufficiently advanced technology is indistinguishable from magic.' - Arthur C. Clarke

civilopediaText.text: Who knows what the future holds?
